title.en: The Anatomy of a Wi-Fi Transmission: Preamble, Header, and Payload
title.zh: 一次 Wi-Fi 发送的解剖：前导码、PHY 头与数据负载

blocks.en: Every Wi-Fi transmission ever sent — from the original 1 Mb/s radios of the 1990s to today's Wi-Fi 7 — is built on one shared idea: a packet on the air is not just your data. Before the bits you actually care about, the radio sends a runway and a label. The whole structure is called a PPDU (PLCP Protocol Data Unit), and learning its three parts once gives you a map you can reuse for every generation of Wi-Fi that follows. We'll use the simplest real example — the original DSSS PHY — because its PPDU is small enough to see whole.
blocks.zh: 迄今为止发出的每一次 Wi-Fi 传输——从 1990 年代最早的 1 Mb/s 电台，到今天的 Wi-Fi 7——都建立在同一个共同的理念之上：空中的一个数据包绝不仅仅是你的数据。在你真正关心的那些比特之前，电台会先发出一段“跑道”和一个“标签”。这整个结构被称为 PPDU（PLCP 协议数据单元），只要把它的三个部分弄明白一次，你就得到了一张可以套用到之后每一代 Wi-Fi 的地图。我们用最简单的真实例子——最初的 DSSS PHY——来讲解，因为它的 PPDU 小到可以一眼看全。

blocks.en: A PPDU has three parts in order. First the PHY preamble: a fixed, known pattern that lets a receiver detect that a frame is starting, lock its clock to the sender, and find where each symbol begins. Second the PHY header (the PLCP header): a small block of control fields that announces how to read what comes next — most importantly the data rate and the length. Third the PSDU: the payload, the actual MAC frame bits being delivered. The receiver processes the preamble and header purely to set itself up to demodulate the payload correctly.
blocks.zh: 一个 PPDU 按顺序分为三部分。第一是 PHY 前导码（preamble）：一段固定的、已知的图案，让接收机能够察觉一帧正在开始、把自己的时钟锁定到发送方，并找出每个符号从哪里起始。第二是 PHY 头（即 PLCP 头）：一小块控制字段，用来宣告“接下来的内容该怎么读”——其中最重要的是数据速率和长度。第三是 PSDU：负载，也就是真正要投递的 MAC 帧比特。接收机处理前导码与 PHY 头，纯粹是为了把自己调校好，从而正确解调负载。

blocks.en: Look closely at the header fields and you can read the receiver's instructions. SYNC is just scrambled 1s — a long, monotonous tone whose only job is to give the receiver something obvious to latch onto. SFD (Start Frame Delimiter) is a single fixed 16-bit pattern, X'F3A0', that says 'the timed fields begin right after me.' SIGNAL carries the data rate (its value times 100 kb/s — so X'0A' means 1 Mb/s DBPSK, X'14' means 2 Mb/s DQPSK). LENGTH says how many microseconds the payload will take. And a CRC-16 protects SIGNAL, SERVICE, and LENGTH so a corrupted header is caught rather than mis-trusted.
blocks.zh: 仔细看 PHY 头里的字段，你就能读出“给接收机的说明书”。SYNC 不过是被加扰后的一串 1——一段冗长、单调的音调，它唯一的任务就是给接收机一个明显的东西去抓住。SFD（帧起始定界符）是一个固定的 16 比特图案 X'F3A0'，意思是“需要精确计时的字段从我之后立即开始”。SIGNAL 携带数据速率（它的值乘以 100 kb/s——所以 X'0A' 表示 1 Mb/s DBPSK，X'14' 表示 2 Mb/s DQPSK）。LENGTH 说明负载将占用多少微秒。而一个 CRC-16 保护着 SIGNAL、SERVICE 和 LENGTH，这样一旦 PHY 头被损坏就能被发现，而不会被错误地信任。

blocks.en: Here is the detail that explains the whole track to come. The entire preamble and header are always sent at the slowest, toughest rate (1 Mb/s DBPSK), no matter how fast the payload is. Why cripple the start of every frame? Because the header is what tells the receiver how to decode the payload — if you can't read the header, the rest is useless. So the part everyone must read is sent in the most robust way possible, and only the payload speeds up once the rate has been announced in SIGNAL.
blocks.zh: 下面这个细节，能够解释整条学习路线后续的全部内容。无论负载多快，整个前导码和 PHY 头永远以最慢、最“皮实”的速率（1 Mb/s DBPSK）发送。为什么要给每一帧的开头“自废武功”？因为正是 PHY 头告诉接收机该如何解码负载——如果你连 PHY 头都读不出来，后面的一切都没有意义。所以“人人都必须读懂”的那一部分，用尽可能稳健的方式发送；只有在 SIGNAL 宣告了速率之后，负载才会提速。

blocks.svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 420 200' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><line x1='30' y1='150' x2='400' y2='150'/><polygon points='400,150 392,146 392,154' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'/><text x='340' y='170' font-size='11' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>bits / symbol</text><line x1='30' y1='150' x2='30' y2='20'/><polygon points='30,20 26,28 34,28' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'/><text x='40' y='30' font-size='11' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>required signal strength</text><line x1='30' y1='150' x2='370' y2='40' stroke-dasharray='5 4'/><circle cx='110' cy='114' r='5' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'/><text x='120' y='118' font-size='11' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>slow, robust, far</text><circle cx='300' cy='70' r='5' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'/><text x='200' y='62' font-size='11' fill='currentColor' stroke='none'>fast, fragile, near</text></svg>

caption.en: The trade-off that drives every Wi-Fi generation: packing more bits into each symbol raises the data rate but demands a stronger, cleaner signal — so it works only at shorter range. Fewer bits per symbol is slower but reaches farther.
caption.zh: 驱动每一代 Wi-Fi 的核心权衡：每个符号塞入更多比特会提高数据速率，但也要求更强、更干净的信号——因此只能在更近的距离上工作。每个符号比特更少则速率更慢，但能传得更远。

blocks.en: That trade-off is the engine of the whole evolution story. DBPSK packs 1 bit per symbol; DQPSK packs 2, doubling the rate to 2 Mb/s — but a 2-bit symbol carries twice as many distinct states, so the receiver needs a cleaner signal to tell them apart, which means it only works closer to the AP or with less interference. Later generations push this far harder with richer modulations, but the principle never changes: more bits per symbol buys speed at the cost of fragility and range. One more housekeeping detail: all transmitted bits are run through a feedthrough scrambler, which breaks up long runs of identical bits (like the SYNC's 1s) so the radio doesn't dwell on a single tone — the descrambler is self-synchronizing, so the receiver needs no prior key.
blocks.zh: 这个权衡正是整个演进故事的引擎。DBPSK 每个符号承载 1 比特；DQPSK 承载 2 比特，把速率翻倍到 2 Mb/s——但一个 2 比特的符号要表示两倍数量的不同状态，于是接收机需要更干净的信号才能把它们区分开，这意味着它只能在离 AP 更近、或干扰更小的情况下工作。后来的各代用更丰富的调制方式把这一点推得更狠，但原理从未改变：每个符号多塞比特，是以脆弱性与传输距离为代价换取速度。还有一个“收尾”细节：所有发送的比特都会经过一个前馈式加扰器，它打散长串相同的比特（比如 SYNC 里那一长串 1），让电台不至于长时间停在单一音调上——解扰器是自同步的，因此接收机无需任何预先约定的密钥。

summary.en: The exact DSSS PPDU layout and why the header is always 1 Mb/s
summary.zh: DSSS PPDU 的精确布局，以及 PHY 头为何始终是 1 Mb/s

blocks.en: Clause 15.3.2 fixes the DSSS PPDU as PHY preamble + PHY header + PSDU (Figure 15-1): a 144-bit preamble (SYNC 128 bits + SFD 16 bits) followed by a 48-bit header (SIGNAL 8 + SERVICE 8 + LENGTH 16 + CRC 16). Clause 15.3.3.1 requires the entire preamble and header to be transmitted using 1 Mb/s DBPSK and all bits to be scrambled. The field definitions specify SYNC as scrambled 1s for synchronization, SFD as the fixed pattern X'F3A0' marking the start of the timed fields, SIGNAL as the data rate (value × 100 kb/s; X'0A' = 1 Mb/s DBPSK, X'14' = 2 Mb/s DQPSK), LENGTH as the PSDU duration in microseconds, and a CRC-16 (polynomial x16 + x12 + x5 + 1) protecting SIGNAL/SERVICE/LENGTH. Clause 15.3.4 defines the self-synchronizing feedthrough scrambler G(z) = z^-7 + z^-4 + 1, and 15.3.5 states the receiver switches to the SIGNAL-indicated modulation starting with the first symbol of the PSDU — so the rate change happens exactly at the preamble/header → payload boundary.
blocks.zh: 15.3.2 节将 DSSS PPDU 固定为“PHY 前导码 + PHY 头 + PSDU”（图 15-1）：一个 144 比特的前导码（SYNC 128 比特 + SFD 16 比特），其后是一个 48 比特的 PHY 头（SIGNAL 8 + SERVICE 8 + LENGTH 16 + CRC 16）。15.3.3.1 节要求整个前导码与 PHY 头都以 1 Mb/s DBPSK 发送，且所有比特都要加扰。字段定义规定：SYNC 为用于同步的加扰 1；SFD 为标记计时字段起始的固定图案 X'F3A0'；SIGNAL 为数据速率（值 × 100 kb/s；X'0A' = 1 Mb/s DBPSK，X'14' = 2 Mb/s DQPSK）；LENGTH 为以微秒计的 PSDU 持续时长；并由一个 CRC-16（多项式 x16 + x12 + x5 + 1）保护 SIGNAL/SERVICE/LENGTH。15.3.4 节定义了自同步的前馈式加扰器 G(z) = z^-7 + z^-4 + 1；15.3.5 节规定接收机从 PSDU 的第一个符号起切换到 SIGNAL 所指示的调制方式——因此速率切换恰好发生在“前导码/PHY 头 → 负载”的边界上。

cites.note: 15.3.2 PPDU format + Figure 15-1 — preamble (SYNC 128b + SFD 16b) + header (SIGNAL/SERVICE/LENGTH/CRC, 48b) + PSDU
15.3.3 PHY field definitions — preamble/header at 1 Mb/s DBPSK; SYNC, SFD X'F3A0', SIGNAL data rate, SERVICE, LENGTH, CRC-16
15.3.4 self-synchronizing feedthrough scrambler G(z)=z^-7+z^-4+1; 15.3.5 modulation/rate change at first PSDU symbol
